article_title: CISA's BOD 26-04 Orders Federal Agencies to Patch the Most Dangerous Vulnerabilities in Three Days, Replacing the CVSS-Score Model

content_preview.title: CISA's BOD 26-04 Orders Federal Agencies to Patch the Most Dangerous Vulnerabilities in Three Days, Replacing the CVSS-Score Model
content_preview.summary: The risk-tiered directive harmonizes BOD 19-02 and BOD 22-01, ranking flaws by exposure, KEV status, automation, and impact.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency has issued Binding Operational Directive 26-04, ordering federal civilian executive branch agencies to patch their highest-risk vulnerabilities within three days. According to [Industrial Cyber](https://industrialcyber.co/cisa/cisa-bod-26-04-directs-agencies-to-prioritize-exploited-vulnerabilities-and-assess-compromise-before-patching/), only the highest risk vulnerabilities must be patched within three days, while vulnerabilities...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured Analysis piece (728 words, within the 800-2000 Analysis range allowing for the Overview/What We Know/What We Don't Know/Analysis framing). Clear separation of reported fact from editorial interpretation; the Analysis section is explicitly labeled and the body never editorializes within the factual sections.
editor_notes.source_verification: All three source snapshots read from disk and integrity-verified (uncompressed sha256 matches manifest for source-0, source-1, source-2). source-0 Industrial Cyber (HTTP 200): confirmed directive title 'Prioritizing Security Updates Based on Risk' verbatim, 'harmonizes and improves BOD 19-02... and BOD 22-01' verbatim, four criteria (Asset Exposure, KEV Status, Exploit Automation, Post-Exploitation Technical Impact) verbatim, 'only the highest risk vulnerabilities must be patched within three days, while vulnerabilities presenting less risk may be remediated over longer timelines' verbatim, 'Applying a patch generally does not evict a threat actor' verbatim, 60-day/180-day staged compliance windows verbatim, June 11 2026 date. source-1 SecurityWeek (HTTP 200): confirmed 'Binding Operational Directive 26-04: Prioritizing Security Updates Based on Risk' verbatim, three-day window for KEV+exposed+automatable assets verbatim, 'remediation timeframe increases to 14 days or 60 days' verbatim, June 11 2026 date. source-2 Help Net Security (HTTP 200): confirmed the four factors verbatim and the CVSS-severity-only-replacement framing ('a meaningful leap forward from CVSS-severity-only patching').
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every claim, the directive code 'BOD 26-04', the directive title, the June 11 2026 date, and the three-day figure all trace verbatim to allowlisted snapshots. No claim rests on cisa.gov (the bot-blocked primary pages are not cited). The article correctly omits the named-official direct quotes from Andersen and Butera/Spring (which appear in Industrial Cyber but were not carried over) and uses Industrial Cyber's exact 'harmonizes and improves' wording rather than any 'supersedes and revokes' paraphrase. No specific KEV CVE IDs are asserted in the body. The internal cross-reference to 2026-05/16-cisco-patches-sixth-sd-wan-zero-day resolves to a real published article.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No sensationalism; the headline's 'Replacing the CVSS-Score Model' is supported by the sources' framing of a move away from severity-only patching. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No prior article covers BOD 26-04. Existing CISA/KEV coverage concerns individual CVEs and zero-days; this is the first coverage of the binding directive itself, and the article correctly frames prior three-day-deadline pieces as the case-by-case precedent the directive generalizes.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully sourced submission. Directive code/title and the three-day figure all trace verbatim to allowlisted snapshots; no unverifiable specifics; no dependence on bot-blocked primary sources. Approved for publication as-is.
